response:
# Perspective Audit — Infinite Scroll Feed With Cognitive Navigation Breakdown ## Step 1 — Escalated Perspectives Confirmed Deep-review checklists run only for perspectives escalated to MEDIUM or HIGH by the a11y-critic: - **Keyboard & Motor Access — HIGH** (reviewed) - **Screen Reader & Semantic Structure — MEDIUM** (reviewed) - **Cognitive & Neurodivergent — HIGH** (reviewed) Perspectives at LOW — **not reviewed** per protocol (Layer 2 fires only where the risk signal exists): Magnification & Reflow; Environmental Contrast; Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity; Auditory Access. See the cross-perspective calibration note — Vestibular & Motion surfaced during this review and may warrant re-escalation, but its checklist was not run. Line references are to the fixture file `infinite-scroll-cognitive-user.md`. --- ## Perspective 5 — Keyboard & Motor Access (HIGH) ### JTBD checklist | Item | WCAG | Result | |---|---|---| | Interactive elements reachable/operable by keyboard | 2.1.1 | PASS for the card links (real ``); but the load-more *functionality* has no discrete keyboard control — see K1 | | No keyboard traps — Tab/Shift+Tab always progress | 2.1.2 | **FINDING K1 (CRITICAL)** | | Skip link present, targets main content | 2.4.1 | **FINDING K2 (MAJOR)** — no bypass of the growing block | | Focus order logical | 2.4.3 | PASS — cards in DOM/reading order; appended content added at end, existing focus preserved | | Focus indicator ≥2px, ≥3:1 contrast | 2.4.7, 2.4.13 | PASS — `.card-body h2 a:focus { outline: 3px solid #005fcc; outline-offset: 2px }` (lines 159–162); #005fcc on white ≈ >3:1 | | Multi-point gestures have single-pointer alternative | 2.5.1 | N/A — no gestures | | Activation on up-event | 2.5.2 | PASS — native anchors | | Visible label matches accessible name | 2.5.3 | PASS — link text = article title = accessible name | | Drag operations have click alternative | 2.5.7 | N/A — no drag | | Targets ≥24×24 CSS px with spacing | 2.5.8 | PASS — heading links are single-line (~21px) but the Spacing exception applies (16px card padding + 1px border + 16px inter-card gap keep 24px target circles from intersecting) | | Content works in portrait and landscape | 1.3.4 | PASS — responsive at 600px (lines 201–204), no orientation lock | ### Red flags - **"Keyboard trap (focus cannot escape a component)" — FIRES → auto-CRITICAL** (K1). - Interactive element unreachable / drag-only / `outline:none` — do not fire. ### Findings **Finding:** Infinite-scroll auto-load creates a keyboard trap. The feed appends 10 new cards each time the sentinel scrolls into view, and reaching the end of the list requires scrolling, so a keyboard-only user can never advance focus *past* the feed to any content that follows it (site footer, etc.). Every Tab toward the bottom regenerates more focusable links ahead of the user. There is no "Load more" button and no way to stop loading. (Each individual Tab does advance focus — the trap is that the component can never be exited.) **WCAG:** 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap (also 2.1.1 Keyboard) **Perspective:** Keyboard & Motor Access **Severity:** CRITICAL (auto-CRITICAL red flag: focus cannot escape the component) **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** lines 36–52 — `loadMore` runs `setCards(prev => [...prev, ...generateCards(...)])`, fired by `IntersectionObserver(entries => { if (entries[0].isIntersecting) loadMore() })` observing `sentinelRef` (line 91). Component comment lines 34–35: "Keyboard users get trapped in infinite Tab sequence." No button control exists anywhere in the JSX. **Fix:** Make loading an explicit, keyboard-operable "Load more" button as the primary trigger (auto-load optional and gated behind it), or cap auto-loads and expose a focusable "continue" control; guarantee focus can always progress to content after the feed. The WAI-ARIA APG Feed pattern with a discrete load affordance is the target design. **Finding:** No mechanism to bypass the continuously growing block of card links. A keyboard user who does not intend to visit every article must still Tab through every link, and the block never terminates. **WCAG:** 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks **Perspective:** Keyboard & Motor Access **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev) **Evidence:** No skip link, no "skip past feed" target, no landmark after the feed inside `
` (lines 70–102); the block grows unbounded (lines 36–52). **Fix:** Add a "Skip feed" link (or land focus on a stable end-of-feed control) and/or move to a bounded pagination / "Load more" model so the block is finite and bypassable. --- ## Perspective 6 — Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (MEDIUM) ### JTBD checklist | Item | WCAG | Result | |---|---|---| | Landmark regions present/correct | 1.3.1 | PASS — `
` present (line 70). See S4 for a separate 1.3.1 relationships gap in card meta | | Heading hierarchy logical, h1 present | 2.4.6 | PASS — h1 "Your Feed" (line 71) → h2 per card (line 81), no skipped levels | | Informative images have alt; decorative alt="" | 1.1.1 | PASS (conditional) — `alt=""` (line 79) is a valid decorative treatment; verify thumbnails carry no info beyond the headline (open question) | | DOM order matches visual reading order | 1.3.2 | PASS — image → body (title, excerpt, meta); appended cards added in order | | Unique descriptive `` | 2.4.2 | N/A from component — document-level (open question) | | Link text describes destination | 2.4.4 | PASS — link text is the article title | | `lang` on `<html>` | 3.1.1 | N/A from component — document-level (open question) | | Foreign-language phrases wrapped | 3.1.2 | N/A — excerpt is Lorem ipsum placeholder | | Inputs have associated `<label>` | 3.3.2 | N/A — no form inputs | | Errors identify field via aria-describedby | 3.3.1 | N/A — no forms/errors | | Custom widgets use correct ARIA roles | 4.1.2 | **FINDING S3 (MINOR)** — redundant `aria-label`; feed semantics absent | | Status messages use `aria-live` appropriately | 4.1.3 | **FINDING S1 (MAJOR)**, **FINDING S2 (MAJOR)** | | SPA route change: title updates, focus managed, announced | — | N/A — infinite load is not a route change; document title unverifiable from component | ### Red flags None fire: `<main>` is present; no unlabeled form inputs; content uses native semantics (`<article>`, `<h2>`, `<a>`) so there is no custom widget without a role; `<html lang>` is document-level and cannot be confirmed absent from this source. Consistent with the MEDIUM (not HIGH) escalation. ### Findings **Finding:** The `aria-live="polite"` loading region is conditionally mounted together with its own text content (`{loading && (<div aria-live="polite"><span className="sr-only">Loading more articles</span>…)}`). A live region inserted into the DOM at the same moment as its content is unreliably announced — many screen reader / browser pairings announce only mutations to a live region that already existed. The "Loading more articles" message will frequently not be spoken. **WCAG:** 4.1.3 Status Messages **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author) **Evidence:** lines 91–98 — the live region and its `sr-only` text are both children of the `{loading && (...)}` conditional; the region does not exist in the DOM until loading begins. **Fix:** Render a persistent, always-present live region (e.g., an empty `<div aria-live="polite" class="sr-only">` outside the conditional) and update only its text when loading starts and stops. **Finding:** No status message is announced when loading *completes* and 10 new articles are appended, and the feed container carries no `aria-busy` / feed semantics. Screen reader users get no reliable indication that content is loading (S1) or that new content arrived, or how much. **WCAG:** 4.1.3 Status Messages (WAI-ARIA APG Feed pattern) **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** lines 45–52 append cards silently; the only live region (lines 92–97) attempts to announce the *start* of loading, never completion; the container is a plain `<div className="feed-container">` (line 76) with no `role="feed"` / `aria-busy`. **Fix:** Announce completion (e.g., "10 more articles loaded") through the persistent live region, and/or adopt the APG Feed pattern: `role="feed"` on the container with `aria-busy="true"` during load flipped to `false` when done, and child `role="article"` with `aria-labelledby` and `aria-posinset` / `aria-setsize`. **Finding:** Each `<article>` sets `aria-label={card.title}`, duplicating the visible `<h2>` link text (also the title). A screen reader exposes the title as the article's accessible name and again at the heading/link — redundant and slightly noisy; `aria-label` on the region is also not the APG-preferred association. **WCAG:** 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** MINOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev **Evidence:** line 78 `<article … aria-label={card.title}>` vs line 81 `<h2><a …>{card.title}</a></h2>`. **Fix:** Reference the heading with `aria-labelledby` (give the `<h2>` an id) instead of duplicating the string; this also satisfies the APG Feed article-labeling requirement. **Finding:** Card metadata (author and timestamp) are bare `<span>`s with no role or label. A screen reader announces "Jane Smith 5m ago" with no cue that these are the author and publication time; the relationships conveyed visually by layout are not conveyed programmatically. **WCAG:** 1.3.1 Info and Relationships **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** MINOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author) **Evidence:** lines 83–86 — `<span className="card-author">…</span> <span className="card-time">…</span>` with no labels. **Fix:** Add visually-hidden labels ("By", "Posted"), or use `<address>` for the author and `<time datetime="…">` for the timestamp so the semantics are exposed. --- ## Perspective 7 — Cognitive & Neurodivergent (HIGH) Note on the HIGH alarm: the component's most acute cognitive concern — inability to tell position or whether the feed ends — maps to **2.4.8 Location, which is AAA** and is therefore capped at ENHANCEMENT by the calibration rules (C3). The actionable **AA** cognitive barriers are C1 and C2 (both MAJOR). Rating the 2.4.8 concern above ENHANCEMENT would violate the protocol regardless of real-world impact. ### JTBD checklist | Item | WCAG | Result | |---|---|---| | Error messages suggest corrective action | 3.3.3 | N/A — no forms/errors | | Multi-step forms don't re-ask info | 3.3.7 | N/A | | Auth: no cognitive test; paste/autofill work | 3.3.8 | N/A | | Navigation in same relative position across pages | 3.2.3 | N/A — single component, no cross-page comparison | | Same-function components have consistent labels | 3.2.4 | PASS — cards uniform | | Help mechanism in same location | 3.2.6 | N/A — no help mechanism | | Focus does not auto-trigger context change | 3.2.1 | PASS — focusing a link changes no context (appended content is a content change, not a WCAG "context" change) | | Changing control value doesn't auto-submit | 3.2.2 | N/A — no form controls | | Destructive actions have undo/confirmation | 3.3.4 | N/A — no destructive actions | | More than one way to reach a page | 2.4.5 | **FINDING C2 (MAJOR)** | | Personal-data inputs have autocomplete | 1.3.5 | N/A — no inputs | | Timeout warnings give ≥20s | 2.2.1 | N/A — no timeout | | Content avoids unexplained jargon | — | N/A from source — excerpt is Lorem ipsum placeholder (open question) | | **Neurodivergent:** predictable layout, no layout shifts | — | **FINDING C1 (MAJOR)** — auto-update + auto-append | | **Neurodivergent:** clear visual hierarchy | — | PASS — h2 + spacing + grouping | | **Neurodivergent:** no autoplay media / unprompted modals | — | PASS — no video/modal (spinner motion is a Vestibular concern; see calibration note) | | **Neurodivergent:** instructions don't rely on memory | — | N/A | | Orientation / knowing where you are and whether it ends | 2.4.8 (AAA) | **FINDING C3 (ENHANCEMENT)** | ### Red flags None fire: no CAPTCHA/cognitive test, no paste-blocked field, no multi-step re-collection, no destructive action without confirmation. The HIGH alarm here reflects disorientation risk, not a red-flag CRITICAL — an honest audit rates the AA barriers as MAJOR and the AAA orientation gap as ENHANCEMENT. ### Findings **Finding:** Auto-updating and auto-appending content with no pause/stop/hide. Relative timestamps re-render every 60 seconds ("5m ago" → "6m ago"), and new cards append automatically on scroll; both change the content and shift scroll position while the user is reading. No control exists to pause, stop, or freeze these updates. Cognitive/neurodivergent users lose their place and are forced to re-read. **WCAG:** 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide **Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev) **Evidence:** lines 54–59 — `setInterval(() => setTick(t => t + 1), 60000)` forces timestamp re-render (comment line 54: "content shifts while reading"); lines 36–52 auto-append with no user action. **Fix:** Provide a mechanism to pause auto-updates (stop the 60s tick, or show static/absolute times with relative time on demand) and gate content loading behind a user action so content does not move under the reader. *Cross-perspective note:* 2.2.2 canonically belongs to the Vestibular & Motion perspective, which was rated LOW / not escalated — see the calibration note; this finding is surfaced by the cognitive review but implicates that alarm. **Finding:** Sequential scrolling is the only way to locate or return to content in the feed — no pagination, search, filter, "back to top," or bookmark/return affordance. Content deep in the feed is reachable only by long auto-loading scroll, and a user who leaves (opens an article, presses Back) typically loses all loaded state and scroll position and must start over. Cognitive users are disproportionately harmed. **WCAG:** 2.4.5 Multiple Ways **Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev) **Evidence:** the component exposes only the scroll-driven feed (lines 70–102); no search/nav/pagination present. **[Site-level verification required — 2.4.5 can be met by global search/navigation elsewhere in the site; confirm whether the surrounding app provides another way to reach these article pages.]** **Fix:** Provide at least one alternative locating mechanism (search, filters, pagination, or sitemap) and preserve/restore scroll position and loaded state on back-navigation. **Finding:** No orientation cues — no position indicator, no page numbers, no "back to top," and no "end of feed" marker — so the user cannot tell where they are in the feed or whether it ends. This is the component's central cognitive concern (flagged in-code as WCAG 2.4.8). **WCAG:** 2.4.8 Location (AAA) **Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent **Severity:** ENHANCEMENT (2.4.8 is AAA — capped at ENHANCEMENT per calibration, regardless of impact) **Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev) **Evidence:** component comments lines 73–74 and 100; no position/end UI in the JSX (lines 70–102) or CSS. **Fix:** Add orientation affordances — "back to top," an item/position count, and a clear end-of-feed marker when no more content exists. High-value even though AAA, because it materially reduces disorientation; treat as a strongly recommended enhancement, not a shipping blocker. --- ## Cross-Perspective Calibration Note Two signals encountered during the Cognitive review implicate **Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity (2.2.2, 2.3.1, 2.5.4)**, which the critic rated LOW and which was therefore **not** deep-reviewed: 1. `.spinner { animation: spin 0.8s linear infinite }` (CSS lines 185–193) with **no `prefers-reduced-motion` guard anywhere** in the stylesheet. 2. Auto-updating timestamps and auto-appending content (finding C1 above), which 2.2.2 governs. Per protocol I did not run the Motion checklist. Recommendation: **re-check the Vestibular & Motion alarm** — the unguarded infinite spin animation plus auto-updating content suggest it may belong at MEDIUM, which would trigger a Motion deep review. This is a calibration flag, not a completed finding. --- ## Perspective Audit Summary **Component/artifact reviewed:** Infinite Scroll Feed With Cognitive Navigation Breakdown (`InfiniteScrollFeed` React component + CSS) **Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Keyboard & Motor Access (HIGH); Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (MEDIUM); Cognitive & Neurodivergent (HIGH) **Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow; Environmental Contrast; Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity *(flagged for re-escalation — see calibration note)*; Auditory Access ### Findings by Severity | Severity | Count | |---|---| | CRITICAL | 1 | | MAJOR | 5 | | MINOR | 2 | | ENHANCEMENT | 1 | ### Perspectives That Passed None — all three escalated perspectives produced findings. ### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role **Front-End Dev** - Infinite-scroll keyboard trap — Keyboard & Motor — CRITICAL — 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap - Live region conditionally mounted with content (unreliable announcement) — Screen Reader — MAJOR — 4.1.3 Status Messages - No completion announcement / no `aria-busy` feed semantics — Screen Reader — MAJOR — 4.1.3 Status Messages - `<article>` `aria-label` duplicates heading text — Screen Reader — MINOR — 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - Card meta (author/time) not programmatically labeled — Screen Reader — MINOR — 1.3.1 Info and Relationships **Visual Design** - (none) **UX Design** - No bypass of the growing feed block — Keyboard & Motor — MAJOR — 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks - Auto-updating/appending content with no pause/stop/hide — Cognitive — MAJOR — 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide - Only sequential scroll to locate/return to content — Cognitive — MAJOR — 2.4.5 Multiple Ways - No position / end-of-feed / back-to-top orientation — Cognitive — ENHANCEMENT — 2.4.8 Location (AAA) **Content Author** - (none as primary owner; secondary on the card-meta and live-region findings, plus the open questions below) ### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification - **Thumbnail images (1.1.1):** `alt=""` (line 79) is valid only if the images are genuinely decorative. Confirm each article thumbnail carries no information beyond the headline; if it does, provide descriptive `alt`. [Content Author] - **Document `<title>` (2.4.2) and `<html lang>` (3.1.1):** not settable in this component. Verify the host app provides a unique, descriptive title and correct `lang`, and updates the title when the feed route is entered. - **Reading level / jargon (Cognitive content check):** the excerpt is Lorem ipsum placeholder; verify real article copy at publication. - **Multiple Ways (2.4.5, C2):** confirm whether global site search/navigation provides another way to reach these article pages; if not, the MAJOR stands unmitigated. ### Recommended Next Step **BLOCK** — one CRITICAL finding (K1, infinite-scroll keyboard trap) blocks access to any content following the feed for keyboard-only users; do not ship until resolved. After the trap is fixed, **REVISE** remains: five MAJOR findings (K2, S1, S2, C1, C2) must be addressed before shipping. Re-run a11y-test (real keyboard walkthrough + live-region announcement capture) after fixes, and re-check the Vestibular & Motion alarm per the calibration note.

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
